_**Agent (default)**_

Here is an overview of **Phase 8** as defined in this project’s [ROADMAP](docs/ROADMAP.md).

## Status & position

| | |
|---|---|
| **Title** | Certificates & outbound stubs |
| **Status** | **Planned** — no `phase-8-*.md` delivery doc yet |
| **Prerequisite** | **Phase 7** (Decision & registration) — cases must reach a completed/registered state before certificates make sense |
| **Maps to IDEA.md** | Phases **21–23** (residence card production, informing other administrations, citizen documents) |

**Naming note:** In [IDEA.md](IDEA.md), “Phase 8” is **Civil Status**. That content was already delivered earlier in this codebase (`RecordCivilStatus`, `CivilStatusStep.razor`, etc.). When the team says **Phase 8** in roadmap terms, they mean **certificates and outbound notifications**, not civil status.

---

## Goal

Deliver **citizen-facing outputs** and **simulated administration notifications** after a registration is confirmed — the “what happens after registration is complete” slice of the Belgian population workflow.

---

## Vertical slices (planned)

| Slice | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `IssueResidenceCertificate` | Generate a residence certificate for a registered person |
| `IssueHouseholdComposition` | Generate a household composition document |
| `ListOutboundNotifications` | View the log of simulated outbound messages to other administrations |

These are described in [DOMAIN.md](docs/DOMAIN.md) under **§9. Certificates & notifications**, triggered by a `RegistrationConfirmed` domain event (from Phase 7).

---

## Domain concepts (not built yet)

- **`CertificateRequest`** — residence certificate, household composition, proof of address
- **`OutboundNotification`** — tax, social security, health insurance, etc. (log-only stubs, no real integrations)

---

## Infrastructure (planned)

- **PDF generation stub** — simple HTML → PDF, or a printable Razor page
- **Notification log table** — persisted record of what would have been sent to other public bodies

---

## UI (planned)

- **“Print certificate”** on completed cases
- **Admin view** of simulated outbound messages (fits the design-system “Administration & settings” template pattern in [06-page-templates.md](docs/design-system/06-page-templates.md))

Related UI work deferred to Phase 8 from earlier phases:

- **Full document viewer template** — print toolbar, dedicated `AppDocumentPreview` wrapper, left-list/right-preview layout (Phase 4.1 shipped a practical subset only)
- **Print CSS** — hide app chrome when printing certificates ([04-mudtheme-and-css.md](docs/design-system/04-mudtheme-and-css.md))
- **Comfortable density** for citizen-facing certificate screens ([07-architecture-and-guidelines.md](docs/design-system/07-architecture-and-guidelines.md))

---

## Demo scenario (from roadmap)

> Registration complete → officer prints a certificate PDF → notification log shows something like **“Notify tax administration.”**

That end-to-end flow depends on Phase 7’s `ConfirmRegistration` / `RegistrationConfirmed` event being in place first.

---

## What exists today vs. what Phase 8 adds

| Already in codebase | Phase 8 would add |
|---------------------|-------------------|
| Civil status intake (`RecordCivilStatus`) | Certificate issuance handlers |
| Document preview on case detail (Phase 4.1) | Full print-oriented document viewer |
| Police verification outbound *request* | Outbound *notification log* after registration |
| Registration case through police verification | Post-registration outputs and admin notifications |

---

## Summary

Phase 8 closes the registration story: once Phase 7 lets an officer approve and confirm registration, Phase 8 produces **printable citizen documents** and records **stub notifications** to other Belgian administrations — teaching the “after registration” part of the workflow without real external integrations.

If you want, I can also outline how Phase 8 would likely break into implementation tasks, or how it connects to Phase 7’s domain events.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Ready to implement phase 8.

Phase 7 is the prerequisite. It adds RegisterTarget and rejection/suspension reasons to the domain. RegistrationCase gets decision fields and methods. RegisterDeterminable logic marks when registration is determinable. A RegistrationConfirmed event and AssignNationalRegisterNumber method are added. Infrastructure updates RegistrationCaseConfiguration.

Phase 7 infrastructure also includes a domain event dispatcher and a migration. Web slices cover review dashboards, approval, rejection, suspension, resumption, and confirmation. The UI adds a review section with action buttons. Phase 8 introduces CertificateType, CertificateRequest, OutboundNotification, and OutboundNotificationRecipient.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A solid plan is ready for phase 8. This is a large implementation.

A `RegisterTargetResolver` will map residence categories to default register targets. EU citizens map to PopulationRegister. Non-EU workers map to ForeignersRegister. Students may use ForeignersRegister or WaitingRegister. `RegisterDeterminable` will be marked once residence category and legal residence are established.

Approve requires identity, legal residence, address confirmation, and a determinable register target. It sets status to Approved and stores the register target. ConfirmRegistration assigns an NR number and raises a RegistrationConfirmed event. Reject sets a rejected status with a reason. Suspend moves the record from intake or under review. Resume returns suspended records to under review.

Resume will return suspended records to UnderReview if police verification is complete, otherwise to Intake. Domain events will be collected by RegistrationCase and dispatched after persistence.

A simpler handler pattern replaces complex event sourcing. ConfirmRegistration will directly invoke `IRegistrationConfirmedHandler` to generate notifications. The implementation uses a lightweight `RegistrationConfirmed` event record, a handler interface, and an outbound notification service.

PDF generation will use a printable Razor page or HTML string instead of an external library. A new `ICertificateDocumentGenerator` interface will return HTML content for browser printing. A certificate domain is being introduced with a `CertificateType` enum covering residence certificates and household composition certificates.

The `CertificateRequest` entity now includes fields for registration case linkage, certificate type, issuance timestamp, issuing officer, and document reference. An `OutboundNotification` entity is also being defined in the Notifications domain.

OutboundNotification will carry a recipient, message, creation timestamp, and a stubbed status field. Notifications will be sent to tax administration, social security, and health insurance. On registration confirmation, three notifications will be generated for these three entities.

RegistrationCase will gain properties for RegisterTarget, rejection reason, suspension reason, registered timestamp, and closed timestamp. A GetReviewDashboard method will compute a four-question review response from case state. The RegisterTarget enum will be placed in Domain/ReferenceData or Domain/Registration. Person.AssignNationalRegisterNumber will mirror the existing NationalRegisterPerson pattern.

The `RegisterTarget` enum now includes `SpecialRegister` alongside the other three values. A static `RegisterTargetRules` class provides the `SuggestFor` method, mapping each residence category to its default register target.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Proceeding with systematic implementation of Phase 8.

Defining core domain entities for Phase 8. Starting with enums for certificate types and notification recipients, plus value objects and the main CertificateRequest entity.

CertificateRequest now includes fields for certificate type, issued person, timestamp, issuing officer, and reference number, plus a static Issue factory method. OutboundNotificationId and the OutboundNotification entity are also being defined.

OutboundNotification now includes recipient, message, and creation timestamp fields, plus a static Create factory method. Two repository interfaces are planned: ICertificateRequestRepository for certificate requests, and IOutboundNotificationRepository for outbound notifications. A RegistrationConfirmedNotificationHandler will generate three notifications for tax administration, social security, and health insurance.

The DI container currently only allows a single `IRegistrationConfirmedHandler` registration. This prevents adding the new notification handler alongside the existing logging handler. The registration pattern needs to change to support multiple handlers.

Both handlers can be registered as scoped services against the same interface. The dispatcher will resolve all instances via `GetServices<IRegistrationConfirmedHandler>()`. An `ICertificateRenderer` interface is being introduced for certificate PDF generation.

`ICertificateRenderer` defines two methods: `RenderResidenceCertificate` and `RenderHouseholdComposition`, each accepting a typed model and returning HTML.

`HtmlCertificateRenderer` in Infrastructure will generate HTML with municipal styling and print CSS.

`IssueResidenceCertificateHandler` requires a Registered case status. It loads the person, address, and NR number, creates a `CertificateRequest` record, and returns HTML content plus a reference number.

`IssueHouseholdCompositionHandler` follows the same pattern but includes household members.

`ListOutboundNotificationsHandler` is also being planned.

POST endpoints will issue residence and household composition certificates and return HTML. A GET endpoint will list outbound notifications. The certificate print page may use a Razor view instead of inline HTML. The demo flow will separate certificate issuance from the printable output.

The certificate endpoint will return HTML directly for printing. A `CertificatesSection.razor` component on the case detail page will contain buttons that open the certificate in a new tab. `IssueResidenceCertificateHandler` will validate the case, persist the request, and return an `IssueCertificateResponse` containing the reference number and HTML content.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking the CaseAuditAction enum for a certificate issued action. Reviewing RegistrationCaseDetail for audit loading.

Certificate reference numbers will use a simple format. Print certificates will be rendered via API endpoints returning HTML in new tabs instead of interactive server components. Starting implementation now.

Settled on a simpler Blazor approach. A dedicated Razor page will handle certificate printing directly. It will call the issue handler on initialization and render the certificate with print CSS and optional auto-print.

Each certificate print request will generate a new record with a fresh reference number. The registration confirmation notification handler needs its own database context scope because the dispatcher runs after the main save operation.

Writing the `RegistrationConfirmedNotificationHandler`. It creates an outbound notification for the tax administration when a registration is confirmed.

The notification handler will also notify social security and health insurance. It saves all three notifications in a single transaction. A CertificateIssued audit action will be added and recorded during certificate issuance. New domain files CertificateType.cs and CertificateRequestId.cs are being created under Certificates.

Defining the CertificateRequest entity and its repository interface. Outbound notification entities, identifiers, and repositories are also being created. Infrastructure mappings and persistence configurations for both domains are being added. A certificate renderer interface is planned for the web layer.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Certificate print pages will use `@layout null` to strip out app chrome. Inline print styles will be embedded directly in the HTML from the renderer.

Creating integration tests for certificates and notifications. Adding a helper to build registered cases. Checking existing repository mappings. Will run migrations after entity creation. Outlining the residence certificate handler structure.

The handler constructor accepts renderer, audit recorder, time provider, and current officer dependencies. The Handle method fetches the case, validates its registered status, retrieves the person and declared address, and records the issuance timestamp.

The handler generates a reference number for the residence certificate. It creates the certificate record and persists it to the repository. It then logs the issuance in the case audit.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Implementing Phase 8: domain entities, infrastructure, handlers, UI, and tests.

_**Agent (default)**_

Phase 8 is implemented. Here's what was added:

## Domain
- **`CertificateRequest`** — tracks issued residence and household composition certificates
- **`OutboundNotification`** — log-only stubs for tax, social security, and health insurance
- **`CaseAuditAction.CertificateIssued`** — audit trail when a certificate is printed

## Infrastructure
- EF tables + migration `CertificatesAndNotifications`
- **`HtmlCertificateRenderer`** — printable HTML certificates (with Print button)
- **`RegistrationConfirmedNotificationHandler`** — on `RegistrationConfirmed`, creates 3 outbound notification records (alongside the existing logging handler)

## API slices
| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `GET /api/registration/cases/{id}/certificates/residence` | Issue + return printable residence certificate |
| `GET /api/registration/cases/{id}/certificates/household-composition` | Issue + return household composition certificate |
| `GET /api/registration/outbound-notifications` | List all notifications (optional `?caseId=` filter) |

## UI
- **`CertificatesSection`** on registered case detail — opens certificates in a new tab
- **`/administration/outbound-notifications`** — admin table of simulated outbound messages
- Nav item **Outbound notifications** (population officer role)

## Demo flow
1. Complete a case through approve → confirm registration (Phase 7)
2. Open **Outbound notifications** — you should see 3 entries including “Notify tax administration…”
3. On the registered case detail, use **Residence certificate** or **Household composition** to print

## Tests
4 new integration tests in `CertificatesAndNotificationsTests.cs` — all **127 tests** pass (55 domain + 72 integration).

If you want, I can add a `phase-8-certificates-and-notifications.md` delivery doc to match the other phases.
